Table summary.
Decision: Routine budgeted purchase limit. Proposed Direction: Allow normal ministry work without individual committee approval, within an approved budget and assigned authority.. Final Value / Decision: $TBD per purchase. Approval Path: Finance review; church approval if required. Owner: Finance Committee.
Decision: Large purchase threshold. Proposed Direction: Require written pre-approval and funding review.. Final Value / Decision: $TBD. Approval Path: Finance recommendation; determine church-vote trigger. Owner: Finance Committee.
Decision: Outside-budget spending. Proposed Direction: Require approval before commitment except a documented emergency.. Final Value / Decision: Any amount / $TBD. Approval Path: Finance; congregation when required. Owner: Finance Committee.
Decision: Over-budget spending. Proposed Direction: Require budget-transfer or exception approval before purchase.. Final Value / Decision: $TBD variance or any overage. Approval Path: Finance; congregation when required. Owner: Finance Committee.
Decision: Contract and recurring commitment limit. Proposed Direction: Review the full annual obligation, renewal terms, and cancellation terms.. Final Value / Decision: $TBD monthly / $TBD annual. Approval Path: Finance; congregation when required. Owner: Finance Committee.
Decision: Emergency authority. Proposed Direction: Permit limited action when delay would cause harm, followed by prompt written review.. Final Value / Decision: $TBD. Approval Path: Named emergency approvers; Finance after-the-fact review. Owner: Finance Committee.
Decision: Reimbursement submission period. Proposed Direction: Require prompt documentation while allowing reasonable ministry circumstances.. Final Value / Decision: TBD days. Approval Path: Finance Committee. Owner: Finance Committee.
Decision: Missing receipt exception. Proposed Direction: Use a signed explanation and secondary evidence; repeated exceptions trigger review.. Final Value / Decision: TBD frequency/limit. Approval Path: Assigned reviewer / Finance Committee. Owner: Finance Committee.
Decision: Cardholder eligibility. Proposed Direction: Assign cards to named people, never shared groups.. Final Value / Decision: TBD roles or individuals. Approval Path: Finance Committee. Owner: Finance Committee.
Decision: Card transaction and account limits. Proposed Direction: Match limits to role and approved budget authority.. Final Value / Decision: $TBD. Approval Path: Finance Committee. Owner: Finance Committee.
Decision: Church-vote trigger. Proposed Direction: Preserve congregational authority for major non-budgeted commitments.. Final Value / Decision: $TBD and/or defined categories. Approval Path: Bylaw and congregational review. Owner: Church leadership / Congregation.

Section: Recommended Review Sequence.

Confirm the current bylaw and annual-budget approval requirements.
Agree on the control model before debating individual dollar amounts.
Complete the decision worksheet in a Finance Committee meeting.
Check that the three policies use the same definitions and approval paths.
Ask the Treasurer, Bookkeeper, ministry leaders, and Pastor to test the proposed workflow against common situations.
Obtain professional review where employment, tax, legal, insurance, banking, or accounting questions arise.
Bring any policy or threshold requiring congregational approval to the church with a concise recommendation.
Record the effective date, owner, and annual review date after adoption.

Section: Questions To Test Before Adoption.

Can a ministry leader understand what they may purchase without calling several people?
Can the Bookkeeper see proof of approval before processing an exception?
Can Finance Committee identify all exceptions during monthly review?
Is the Treasurer providing meaningful oversight without becoming the only gatekeeper?
Are recurring contracts evaluated based on their full commitment rather than one monthly payment?
Does the policy clearly return major matters to the congregation?
Are emergency provisions narrow enough to prevent routine bypassing of normal approval?

Needs review against the KBC Constitution and Bylaws before final approval.

End of chapter: Financial Authority Review Package.
